metadata.title: Inside a Plant Cell
metadata.summary: Students inspect a labelled micrograph-style diagram and connect each organelle's structure with its function.
metadata.subject: science
metadata.learningObjective: Identify major plant-cell structures and explain how each structure contributes to the cell's function.

components.text: Which structures keep a plant cell working?
Tap every highlighted region. For each one, explain the link between what the structure is like and what it enables the cell to do.

components.prompt: Select an organelle to reveal its role, then compare it with a neighbouring structure.
components.altText: A rectangular plant cell diagram with a thick outer cell wall, thin cell membrane, large central vacuole, nucleus and several green chloroplasts in cytoplasm.

hotspots.label: Cell wall
hotspots.reveal: The cellulose cell wall is rigid. It supports the cell, helps it keep its shape and resists the pressure created when water enters.

hotspots.label: Cell membrane
hotspots.reveal: The partially permeable cell membrane controls which substances enter and leave the cell.

hotspots.label: Large central vacuole
hotspots.reveal: The vacuole contains cell sap. When filled with water, it pushes outwards and helps keep the plant cell turgid.

hotspots.label: Nucleus
hotspots.reveal: The nucleus contains genetic material and controls many of the cell's activities.

hotspots.label: Chloroplast
hotspots.reveal: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll, which absorbs light energy for photosynthesis.
